summary.name: Long-Only Liquid Equities Tail-Risk Optimized
summary.author: Louis Szeto
summary.description: The strategy runs a long only, high turnover equity allocation that assumes broad short horizon positive drift across the most liquid US listed stocks, and seeks to convert that weak directional signal into a risk efficient portfolio via tail risk optimization. summary.description_full:
The strategy runs a long only, high turnover equity allocation that assumes broad short horizon positive drift across the most liquid US listed stocks, and seeks to convert that weak directional signal into a risk efficient portfolio via tail risk optimization. Each day it forms a universe of the 10 most liquid equities meeting basic investability constraints (price above 10 and dollar volume above 10 million) and generates 1 day bullish return forecasts for every name, so security selection is driven entirely by liquidity while weights are driven by relative risk and recent return behavior rather than differentiated alpha views.

Portfolio weights are re optimized daily using a mean minus CVaR objective: expected returns are estimated from recent daily returns, and the optimizer maximizes expected return while penalizing 5 percent left tail losses (CVaR) with a fixed risk aversion, subject to fully invested, long only, and per asset weight bounds between 0 and 100 percent. Positions are scaled to sum to 100 percent notional and are rebalanced to the new optimized weights each day; effective risk control comes from diversification and explicit tail loss constraints rather than stop losses or discretionary exits.

raw.name: Long-Only Liquid Equities Tail-Risk Optimized

author.name: Louis Szeto
author.bio: Associate of the Society of Actuaries. MSc Actuarial Science; MSc Financial Engineering. Former QC Quant Dev.

raw.description:
The strategy runs a long only, high turnover equity allocation that assumes broad short horizon positive drift across the most liquid US listed stocks, and seeks to convert that weak directional signal into a risk efficient portfolio via tail risk optimization. Each day it forms a universe of the 10 most liquid equities meeting basic investability constraints (price above 10 and dollar volume above 10 million) and generates 1 day bullish return forecasts for every name, so security selection is driven entirely by liquidity while weights are driven by relative risk and recent return behavior rather than differentiated alpha views.

Portfolio weights are re optimized daily using a mean minus CVaR objective: expected returns are estimated from recent daily returns, and the optimizer maximizes expected return while penalizing 5 percent left tail losses (CVaR) with a fixed risk aversion, subject to fully invested, long only, and per asset weight bounds between 0 and 100 percent. Positions are scaled to sum to 100 percent notional and are rebalanced to the new optimized weights each day; effective risk control comes from diversification and explicit tail loss constraints rather than stop losses or discretionary exits.
